caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs). The page explains that RMDs are minimum amounts that retirement plan and IRA account owners must withdraw annually after reaching age 72. It specifies that account holders can delay their first RMD until April 1 following the year they turn 72 or retire, depending on the type of retirement plan. The text highlights that RMDs are taxable income and may incur penalties if not taken timely. For traditional IRAs, SEP, SARSEP, and SIMPLE IRAs, distributions must start at age 72, even if the individual is still working. Account holders turning 72 in 2022 must take their first RMD by April 1, 2023, and subsequent RMDs by December 31 each year thereafter. The video also mentions similar rules for 401(k), 403(b), and 457(b) plans, profit-sharing plans, and defined benefit plans, with the first RMD due by April 1 of the later of the year they reach age 72 or leave employment. The cursor is visible, indicating interaction with the webpage.
